target.name: Mission Zero Technologies
target.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mission Zero Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, and Gaël Gobaille-Shaw, which has raised £21.8M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private peers and one public company (LNZA) in the climate and energy sector.

The peer-median method, the primary applicable approach given available data, anchors valuation to three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M. The median of this set is $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method reflects current market pricing for Series A climate technology companies at equivalent development stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median of $150M. The relatively tight clustering of peer valuations—all within the $110M–$200M band—provides reasonable confidence in the central estimate, though execution risk around technology maturation and market adoption timelines remains material. A single method constrains precision; additional data on revenue multiples, funding efficiency, or technology differentiation would strengthen conviction. The $22M raised to date implies a post-money of approximately $150M is consistent with typical Series A dilution patterns in the sector.
